SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.mediagroup
Interface MediaGroup

All Superinterfaces:
AllocationEventNotifier, Joinable, JoinableContainer, JoinEventNotifier, MediaObject, ResourceContainer, java.io.Serializable
public interface MediaGroup
extends JoinableContainer, ResourceContainer

Adapter class which provides the asynchronous access to media containers (files) and signals (DMTF's).

A MediaGroup is initially unrelated to any call. Then, a MediaGroup can be joined/unjoined to another Joinable: a NetworkConnection, or a MediaMixer.

Most of its methods are documented in classes and interfaces defined in contained features :
Player, Recorder, SignalDetector, SignalGenerator. The access to these methods depends on the resource given in the MediaGroupConfig.

Due to the asynchronous nature of the API, the media resources methods (of interfaces Player, Recorder, SignalDetector and SignalGenerator) are non-blocking and the result is passed back to the application as an event through a listener interface (Callback methods). For example, in the play(). method, the result of the play is passed as a PlayerEvent event in a call to onEvent().

Additional/optional capacities include HTTP, ASR, TTS, RTSP ...

Field Detail

PLAYER

static final Configuration<MediaGroup> PLAYER
Defines a MediaGroupConfig containing only a Player interface.
Suitable for ring back tone applications, for example.

This Configuration must support (at least) the following Parameters:

  • Player.MAX_DURATION
  • Player.INTERVAL
  • Player.REPEAT_COUNT
  • Player.START_OFFSET
  • PlayerEvent.PLAY_COMPLETED
It must also support the following Qualifiers:
  • PlayerEvent.END_OF_PLAY_LIST

SIGNALDETECTOR

static final Configuration<MediaGroup> SIGNALDETECTOR
Defines a MediaGroupConfig containing only a SignalDetector.

This Configuration must support (at least) the following Parameters:

  • SignalDetector.MAX_DURATION
  • SignalDetector.INITIAL_TIMEOUT
  • SignalDetector.INTER_SIG_TIMEOUT
  • SignalDetector.PATTERN[0..31]

PLAYER_SIGNALDETECTOR

static final Configuration<MediaGroup> PLAYER_SIGNALDETECTOR
Defines a MediaGroupConfig containing a Player and a SignalDetector.
Suitable for interactive applications that do not need recording.

It must support (at least) the combination of features of PLAYER and SIGNALDETECTOR.

PLAYER_RECORDER_SIGNALDETECTOR

static final Configuration<MediaGroup> PLAYER_RECORDER_SIGNALDETECTOR
A MediaGroupConfig containing Player, Recorder, and SignalDetector.
Suitable for Interactive Voice Response applications.
May contain also a speechdetector, to control begin and end of recording (see SpeechDetectorConstants).

This Configuration must support the items supported by PLAYER_SIGNALDETECTOR, plus (at least) :

  • Recorder.MAX_DURATION
  • Recorder.START_BEEP

PLAYER_RECORDER_SIGNALDETECTOR_SIGNALGENERATOR

static final Configuration<MediaGroup> PLAYER_RECORDER_SIGNALDETECTOR_SIGNALGENERATOR
A MediaGroupConfig containing Player, Recorder, SignalDetector, and SignalGenerator.
Supports all what PLAYER_RECORDER_SIGNALDETECTOR supports, plus the SignalGenerator interface.

Useful for advanced applications, or test applications that use DTMFs for synchronization or continuity checking.

SIGDET_STOPRECORD

static final RTC SIGDET_STOPRECORD
The common RTC to stop a recording when a DTMF is detected.

SIGDET_STOPPLAY

static final RTC SIGDET_STOPPLAY
The common RTC to stop a prompt when a DTMF is detected.

Method Detail

getPlayer

Player getPlayer()
                 throws MsControlException
Returns the Player of this MediaGroup.

Shortcut for getResource(Player.class);

Throws:
MsControlException - when this MediaGroup contains no Player.

getRecorder

Recorder getRecorder()
                     throws MsControlException
Returns the Recorder of this MediaGroup.

Shortcut for getResource(Recorder.class);

Throws:
MsControlException - when this MediaGroup contains no Recorder.

getSignalDetector

SignalDetector getSignalDetector()
                                 throws MsControlException
Returns the SignalDetector of this MediaGroup.

Shortcut for getResource(SignalDetector.class);

Throws:
MsControlException - when this MediaGroup contains no SignalDetector.

getSignalGenerator

SignalGenerator getSignalGenerator()
                                   throws MsControlException
Returns the SignalGenerator of this MediaGroup.

Shortcut for getResource(SignalGenerator.class);

Throws:
MsControlException - when this MediaGroup contains no SignalGenerator.

stop

void stop()
Stops all operations currently in progress on this media group.

Those operations that are stopped get a completion event. Refer to each individual resource with an operation that can be stopped for more details.

See Also:
Player.stop(boolean), Recorder.stop(), SignalDetector.stop()
